Risk: Database schema changes break existing data
Mitigation: Comprehensive migration testing, backup/restore procedures, rollback plan. Test migrations on staging first.

Risk: Page builder performance issues with complex pages
Mitigation: Implement lazy loading, virtualization, progressive rendering. Benchmark regularly. Set complexity limits.

Risk: Staging/production sync failures
Mitigation: Automated testing of promotion workflow, clear rollback procedures, manual verification checklist.

Risk: Security vulnerabilities in user-generated content
Mitigation: Input sanitization, CSP headers, XSS prevention. Security audit before launch. Regular dependency updates.

Risk: React DnD performance on large pages (100+ blocks)
Mitigation: Virtualization, chunking, progressive loading. Test with large pages early. Set recommended limits.

Risk: Users find page builder too complex
Mitigation: User testing throughout development, onboarding tour, video tutorials. Start with 3-5 beta testers.

Risk: Form builder lacks required field types
Mitigation: Extensible architecture for quick field addition. Gather requirements early. Start with 10 common types.

Risk: Migration from hardcoded pages too difficult
Mitigation: Create migration scripts, comprehensive documentation, migration support. Test with 2-3 pages first.

Risk: Staging environment not used properly by team
Mitigation: Enforce workflow through process, training. Make staging mandatory step. Clear guidelines.

Risk: Development takes longer than 12 weeks
Mitigation: Phased rollout, strict MVP scope control. Weekly progress reviews. Cut non-critical features if needed.

Risk: Infrastructure costs increase significantly
Mitigation: Cost monitoring, budget alerts set at $100/month. Optimize queries and caching. Scale gradually.

Risk: Competition from established CMS platforms
Mitigation: Focus on niche (internal intranets), leverage unique integrations (Notion, Workspace). Not competing directly.

Risk: User adoption is slow after launch
Mitigation: Training sessions, documentation, onboarding tour. Identify champions. Regular check-ins.

Risk: Technical debt accumulates during rapid development
Mitigation: Code reviews, maintain test coverage, document decisions. Allocate 20% time for refactoring in Phase 6.

Risk: Key team member becomes unavailable
Mitigation: Document everything in Notion, pair programming, knowledge sharing. Have backup person for critical areas.

Risk: Third-party service outages (Supabase, GCP)
Mitigation: Monitor service status, have fallback plans. Use multiple availability zones. Cache aggressively.

Risk: API rate limits hit (Notion API, Google APIs)
Mitigation: Implement caching, request throttling. Monitor usage. Have upgrade plan ready.

Risk: Browser compatibility issues
Mitigation: Test on all major browsers early. Use polyfills. Define minimum supported browser versions.

Risk: Insufficient test coverage leads to bugs
Mitigation: 90% coverage requirement for critical paths. E2E tests for all workflows. Automated testing in CI/CD.

Risk: Data loss due to bugs or user error
Mitigation: Version history, regular backups, point-in-time recovery. Test restore procedures. Soft delete.

Risk: Scope creep adds features beyond MVP
Mitigation: Strict MVP definition, change control process. Say no to non-critical features. Defer to v2.

Risk: Poor documentation leads to support burden
Mitigation: Allocate sufficient time for docs in Phase 6. User test documentation. Video tutorials.

Risk: Performance degrades over time (data growth)
Mitigation: Database query optimization, proper indexing. Monitor performance metrics. Regular maintenance.

Risk: Deployment failures cause downtime
Mitigation: Blue-green deployment, health checks, automatic rollback. Test deployment process on staging multiple times.

Risk: Supabase limits hit (storage, bandwidth)
Mitigation: Monitor usage against plan limits. Have upgrade path. Consider self-hosting if needed.
